sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: SPACE.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 179, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, SPACE, MOUSE, ACTION7.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: Level reset to initial state. Black F [15,18]-[23,26], Charcoal F [15,36]-[23,44], Yellow F [45,51]-[53,59]. Moving any F shape right→left across divider causes game over. Moving black F left→right across divider swaps with charcoal F (if at same row range).
- Goal model: Uncertain. Need to test SPACE/ACTION7 at initial state.
- Plan: Try pressing SPACE at initial state to see if it completes the level.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 178 actions; a strong score needs about 32 or fewer. You are 5.6x over the target.
NET-ZERO WASTE: your last 177 actions returned the board to a state already seen 177 actions ago — that round-trip made no progress yet still cost 177 actions. Do not extend-and-retract or oscillate; a sequence that ends where it began is pure waste.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The state is confirmed as the initial state:
- Black F: [15,18]-[23,26] (left panel)
- Charcoal F: [15,36]-[23,44] (right panel)
- Yellow F: [45,51]-[53,59] (right panel)
SPACE only changed the timer bar (1 cell at [0,63] or similar). It didn't affect gameplay objects.
Let me commit to a clear plan. My best hypothesis is:
1. The goal is to get the black F to overlap with the yellow F
2. The sequence is: DOWN 30 to align vertically, RIGHT to cross divider and swap with charcoal F, then RIGHT more to overlap with yellow F
But I need to be careful about the exact number of RIGHT moves. Let me calculate:
- After DOWN 30: Black F at [45,18]-[53,26], Charcoal F at [45,36]-[53,44]
- To cross the divider (cols 30-32), the black F needs to move RIGHT until it crosses col 30.
- Black F left edge is at col 18. To reach col 30, need RIGHT 12.
- But the swap happens when crossing the divider. After swap, black F would be at charcoal F's position [45,36]-[53,44].
- Then to overlap with yellow F at [45,51]-[53,59], need RIGHT 15 (from col 36 to col 51).
Wait, but I need to think about what happens when the black F crosses the divider. The swap might happen at a specific point. Let me think about this more carefully.
When the black F is at [45,18]-[53,26] and moves RIGHT:
- RIGHT 1: [45,19]-[53,27]
- RIGHT 2: [45,20]-[53,28]
- ...
- RIGHT 12: [45,30]-[53,38]
At RIGHT 12, the black F is at [45,30]-[53,38], which overlaps with the divider (cols 30-32). This is when the swap would happen. After the swap, the black F would be at the charcoal F's position [45,36]-[53,44].
Wait, but the charcoal F is also moving. When I move RIGHT, the charcoal F moves LEFT. So:
- Initial: Black F [45,18]-[53,26], Charcoal F [45,36]-[53,44]
- RIGHT 1: Black F [45,19]-[53,27], Charcoal F [45,35]-[53,43]
- RIGHT 2: Black F [45,20]-[53,28], Charcoal F [45,34]-[53,42]
- ...
- RIGHT 12: Black F [45,30]-[53,38], Charcoal F [45,24]-[53,32]
At RIGHT 12, the black F is at [45,30]-[53,38] and the charcoal F is at [45,24]-[53,32]. The charcoal F has crossed the divider from right to left! This would cause game over!
So the problem is that when I move RIGHT, the charcoal F moves LEFT and crosses the divider, causing game over. This is what happened earlier when I tried RIGHT from the top-left state.
So I can't just move RIGHT to cross the divider because the charcoal F would also move and cross the divider from right to left, causing game over.
Wait, but earlier I noted that moving RIGHT from the initial state (before DOWN 30) caused a swap without game over. Let me re-examine that.
From the initial state:
- Black F: [15,18]-[23,26] (left panel)
- Charcoal F: [15,36]-[23,44] (right panel)
If I move RIGHT:
- RIGHT 1: Black F [15,19]-[23,27], Charcoal F [15,35]-[23,43]
- RIGHT 2: Black F [15,20]-[23,28], Charcoal F [15,34]-[23,42]
- ...
- RIGHT 12: Black F [15,30]-[23,38], Charcoal F [15,24]-[23,32]
At RIGHT 12, the charcoal F is at [15,24]-[23,32], which overlaps with the divider (cols 30-32). The charcoal F has crossed the divider from right to left. Would this cause game over?
Actually, I think the swap happens before the charcoal F crosses the divider. When the black F reaches the divider (at around RIGHT 12), the swap happens. After the swap, the black F is on the right panel and the charcoal F is on the left panel. So the charcoal F doesn't cross the divider from right to left; instead, it's teleported to the left panel.
But earlier, when I tried RIGHT from the state where black F was at [0,0]-[8,8] and charcoal F was at [0,54]-[8,62], the game over happened after only 3 RIGHT moves. Let me think about why.
From that state:
- Black F: [0,0]-[8,8] (left panel)
- Charcoal F: [0,54]-[8,62] (right panel)
If I move RIGHT:
- RIGHT 1: Black F [0,1]-[8,9], Charcoal F [0,53]-[8,61]
- RIGHT 2: Black F [0,2]-[8,10], Charcoal F [0,52]-[8,60]
- RIGHT 3: Black F [0,3]-[8,11], Charcoal F [0,51]-[8,59]
At RIGHT 3, the charcoal F is at [0,51]-[8,59], which is still on the right panel. The black F is at [0,3]-[8,11], still on the left panel. Neither has crossed the divider. So why did game over happen?
Wait, maybe the game over was triggered by the charcoal F overlapping with the yellow F. The charcoal F is at [0,51]-[8,59] and the yellow F is at [45,51]-[53,59]. They're at different row ranges, so they don't overlap.
Hmm, I'm confused. Let me just try a different approach. Instead of trying to understand the exact mechanics, let me try a sequence that I think should work and see what happens.
My plan:
1. DOWN 30 to align black F with yellow F vertically
2. RIGHT 13 to cross divider and swap with charcoal F (hoping the swap happens before charcoal F crosses divider)
3. RIGHT 15 to overlap with yellow F
But I'm worried about game over. Let me try a safer approach first: just DOWN 30 and see what happens.
Actually, wait. Let me reconsider the game over condition. Maybe the game over happens when the charcoal F crosses the divider from right to left, but ONLY if the black F hasn't swapped yet. In other words, the swap happens when the black F reaches the divider, and after the swap, the charcoal F is on the left panel, so it doesn't cross the divider from right to left.
From the initial state:
- Black F: [15,18]-[23,26] (left panel)
- Charcoal F: [15,36]-[23,44] (right panel)
The black F needs to move RIGHT 12 to reach the divider (col 30). At that point, the charcoal F would have moved LEFT 12, from col 36 to col 24. The charcoal F is now at [15,24]-[23,32], which overlaps with the divider. But the swap happens at the same time, so the charcoal F is teleported to the left panel.
Wait, but the charcoal F is already on the left panel after moving LEFT 12. It's at [15,24]-[23,32], which is on the left panel (cols 0-29) except for cols 30-32 which are the divider. So the charcoal F is partially on the divider.
I think the swap happens when the black F first touches the divider. At that point, the black F and charcoal F swap positions. After the swap, the black F is at the charcoal F's original position and the charcoal F is at the black F's original position.
But this is getting too complicated. Let me just try the sequence and see what happens.
Actually, I realize I should try a much simpler approach. What if the goal is just to press SPACE when the black F is overlapping with the yellow F? Or what if the goal is to press ACTION7 when the black F is overlapping with the yellow F?
Let me try a different hypothesis: the goal is to get the black F to the same position as the yellow F, and then the level completes automatically (no need to press SPACE or ACTION7).
To do this, I need to:
1. Move the black F down to align with the yellow F
2. Move the black F right to cross the divider and end up at the yellow F's position
But as I discussed, moving RIGHT causes the charcoal F to move LEFT, which might cause game over.
Let me try a different approach: what if I move the charcoal F away from the divider first, then move the black F right?
From the initial state:
- Black F: [15,18]-[23,26] (left panel)
- Charcoal F: [15,36]-[23,44] (right panel)
If I move LEFT, the black F moves left and the charcoal F moves right. The charcoal F is at col 36-44, so moving right would put it further from the divider. This is safe.
But moving LEFT would also move the black F left, away from the divider. So I'd need to move RIGHT later to bring the black F back to the divider.
Hmm, this is getting complicated. Let me just try the sequence DOWN 30, RIGHT 13 and see what happens. If it causes game over, I'll know that the charcoal F crossing the divider is the issue.
Actually, I just realized something. The initial state has the black F at [15,18]-[23,26] and the charcoal F at [15,36]-[23,44]. The black F is 9 columns wide (cols 18-26) and the charcoal F is 9 columns wide (cols 36-44). The divider is at cols 30-32.
When I move RIGHT, the black F moves right and the charcoal F moves left. The black F's right edge is at col 26, and the divider starts at col 30. So the black F needs to move RIGHT 4 to touch the divider (right edge at col 30).
But the charcoal F's left edge is at col 36, and the divider ends at col 32. So the charcoal F needs to move LEFT 4 to touch the divider (left edge at col 32).
So after RIGHT 4:
- Black F: [15,22]-[23,30] (right edge at col 30, touching divider)
- Charcoal F: [15,32]-[23,40] (left edge at col 32, touching divider)
At this point, both F shapes are touching the divider. The swap would happen here. After the swap:
- Black F: [15,32]-[23,40] (right panel)
- Charcoal F: [15,22]-[23,30] (left panel)
Wait, but the charcoal F is at [15,32]-[23,40], which overlaps with the divider (cols 30-32). And the black F is at [15,22]-[23,30], which also overlaps with the divider. This is confusing.
Let me just try RIGHT 4 from the initial state and see what happens.
Actually, I've been overthinking this. Let me just commit to a plan and execute it. I'll try:
1. DOWN 30 to align vertically
2. RIGHT 4 to cross the divider and swap
If this causes game over, I'll know the issue and adjust.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Initial state confirmed. Black F [15,18]-[23,26], Charcoal F [15,36]-[23,44], Yellow F [45,51]-[53,59]. SPACE only affects timer bar.
Goal model: Get black F to overlap with yellow F.
Plan: DOWN 30 to align vertically, then RIGHT 4 to cross divider and swap with charcoal F. Test this first.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Test: DOWN 30 then RIGHT 4
# DOWN 30 aligns black F with yellow F vertically
# RIGHT 4 should cross divider and swap with charcoal F
action(['DOWN']*30 + ['RIGHT']*4)
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

When I move RIGHT, the black F moves right and the charcoal F moves left. The black F's right edge is at col 26, and the divider starts at col 30. So the black F needs to move RIGHT 4 to touch the divider (right edge at col 30).
But the charcoal F's left edge is at col 36, and the divider ends at col 32. So the charcoal F needs to move LEFT 4 to touch the divider (left edge at col 32).
So after RIGHT 4:

At this point, both F shapes are touching the divider. The swap would happen here. After the swap:

- Charcoal F: [15,22]-[23,30] (left panel)
